response.justification: The candidate comfortably exceeds every technical requirement — strong TypeScript/Node.js and React experience, REST and web architecture depth, Kubernetes and AWS exposure, and current hands-on LLM/MCP agent work that matches the nice-to-haves. However, he is a staff-level engineer with roughly 15+ years of experience applying to an entry-level position, which raises real questions about compensation alignment, retention, and role satisfaction. His ecological mapping work (GISCollective, Green Map) suggests genuine mission alignment with renewable energy, so a conversation to understand his motivation is warranted, but he is not a natural fit for the seniority band as scoped.
strengths: Deep TypeScript, Node.js, and React production experience directly matching the core stack
Nice-to-haves already covered: LLM/MCP integration work, Kubernetes, AWS (CDK), CI/CD
Demonstrated mission alignment with sustainability through long-running open-source geo/eco projects
concerns: Significantly overqualified for an entry-level role, posing retention and salary-fit risk
No explicit PostgreSQL or Nest.js experience (MongoDB/MySQL and other frameworks instead)
Basic German may limit collaboration in a high on-site-presence German construction/energy context

key_factors.factor: Complete coverage of required tech stack
